summary.en: SMN1 · Homo sapiens (Human) · RNA Binding · Q16637

keywords: Survival motor neuron protein (SMN1) is a protein from Homo sapiens (Human), in the RNA Binding category, assigned to the SMN family. The SMN complex catalyzes the assembly of small nuclear ribonucleoproteins (snRNPs), the building blocks of the spliceosome, and thereby plays an important role in the splicing of cellular pre-mRNAs. Most spliceosomal snRNPs contain a common set of Sm proteins SNRPB

Hemoglobin subunit beta (HBB) is a protein from Homo sapiens (Human), in the Transport category, assigned to the globin family.  Clinically, defects in HBB cause Sickle-cell disease and beta-thalassemia.

Phenylalanine-4-hydroxylase (PAH) is a protein from Homo sapiens (Human), in the Enzyme category, assigned to the biopterin-dependent aromatic amino acid hydroxylase family.  Clinically, defects in PAH cause Phenylketonuria (PKU).

Beta-hexosaminidase subunit alpha (HEXA) is a protein from Homo sapiens (Human), in the Enzyme category, assigned to the glycosyl hydrolase 20 family. Hydrolyzes the non-reducing end N-acetyl-D-hexosamine and/or sulfated N-acetyl-D-hexosamine of glycoconjugates, such as the oligosaccharide moieties from proteins and neutral glycolipids, or from certain mucopolysaccharides. Dystrophin (DMD) is a protein from Homo sapiens (Human), in the Structural category, assigned to the Dystrophin family. Anchors the extracellular matrix to the cytoskeleton via F-actin. Ligand for dystroglycan. Clinically, defects in DMD cause Duchenne and Becker muscular dystrophy.
